text:
Use an interactive rebase going back 3 commits:

```bash
git rebase -i HEAD~3
```

This opens an editor listing the 3 commits, oldest first:

```
pick a1b2c3d First commit
pick d4e5f6g Second commit
pick h7i8j9k Third commit
```

Change `pick` to `squash` (or `s`) on the **lower two** lines, keeping `pick` on the first:

```
pick a1b2c3d First commit
squash d4e5f6g Second commit
squash h7i8j9k Third commit
```

Save and close. Git then opens a second editor to write the combined commit message. Edit it, save, and you're left with one commit.

A faster alternative if you just want to collapse them without the interactive menu — soft-reset and recommit:

```bash
git reset --soft HEAD~3
git commit -m "Your combined message"
```

This moves the branch pointer back 3 commits but keeps all the changes staged, so the single new commit contains everything.

Two cautions: if you've already pushed these commits, you'll need `git push --force-with-lease` afterward, which rewrites shared history — only do that if no one else is building on the branch. And `HEAD~3` must not reach past the start of your branch or a merge commit, or the rebase will pull in more than you intend.

promptText: How do I squash my last 3 commits into one?
